original_abstract: US government research on the health effects of exposure to radiation from nuclear weapons in-volved gross violations of the human rights of peo-ple in the Marshall Islands. In all likelihood, fallout from US nuclear weapons testing in the Pacific was deposited on other Micronesian islands. The US government should release the classified data it pos-sesses on the history of nuclear testing, fallout pat-terns, and the effects on the people of the Marshall Islands and the rest of Micronesia. Health workers should work to ensure that nuclear-armed states ful-fill their treaty obligations to pursue negotiations toward the elimination of nuclear weapons. As a matter of social justice, in order to ensure the human right to health, the federal government should ex-tend Medicaid eligibility to all Micronesian people from the Compact of Free Association nations.
humanized_abstract: U.S. research on the effects of radiation from nuclear bombs on human health was accomplished through egregious violations of the human rights of Marshall Islanders. Further, it is very likely that fallout from U.S. nuclear bomb tests was carried by the wind onto other islands of Micronesia. It is urgently necessary that the classified documents concerning the testing, patterns of fallout, and effects on the health of people of the Marshall Islands and Micronesia be disclosed. Health professionals must also work to strengthen the effort of nuclear-armed countries to honor their obligations of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons in their commitment to negotiate nuclear disarmament. As a matter of justice, for the basic human right of health, the U.S. should extend Medicaid to citizens of Compact of Free Association countries in Micronesia.
